lines: India versus Pakistan. By the numbers, who really comes out ahead?
Population isn't close. India has about 1 point 45 billion people. Pakistan has about 251 million.
GDP is another huge gap. India is near 3 point 9 trillion dollars. Pakistan is around 372 billion.
Land area also goes to India. About 3 point 29 million square kilometers versus 882 thousand.
Life expectancy favors India too. Roughly 72 years versus 68.
Military spending is where the gap becomes dramatic. India spends about 86 billion dollars. Pakistan spends around 10 billion.
Internet access is improving in both, but India still leads. About 70 percent versus 57 percent.
The closest category is nuclear warheads. India is estimated near 172. Pakistan around 170. That's basically neck and neck.
So if you're scoring the big national numbers, India wins clearly. But the nuclear balance means the rivalry stays serious.

stats.label: GDP
stats.note: India's economy is roughly ten times larger.

stats.label: Land Area
stats.note: More territory means more strategic depth.

stats.label: Military Spending
stats.note: This is where the gap becomes dramatic.

stats.label: Internet Users
stats.note: India also leads in digital reach.

stats.label: Nuclear Warheads
stats.note: This one is effectively a dead heat.

seo.title: India vs Pakistan — Who ACTUALLY Wins? 🇮🇳🇵🇰
seo.description:
India vs Pakistan by the numbers: population, GDP, land area, life expectancy, military spending, internet access, and the nuclear balance.

This Datavault short compares the biggest measurable gaps without politics — just the numbers.
